programme.title: JAG: Psychic Warrior
programme.desc: When a Navy research project involving ESP and psychic phenomenon goes awry, resulting in the death of one of the research subjects, the Admiral in charge is court-martialed.

programme.title: JAG: Rouge
programme.desc: As the lowest ranking lawyer at JAG, Bud is assigned to accompany Raglan, a former Special Forces operative who is hired by the Pentagon to test security at military bases.

programme.title: JAG: Mishap
programme.desc: "Skates", Harm's RIO when he was aboard the USS Patrick Henry, was the Landing Safety Officer in charge of the deck when a Tomcat crashes while making an emergency landing.

programme.title: JAG: Into the Breach
programme.desc: Harm and Mac agree to help guide young student "attorneys" at Iron Forge Naval Academy, a prep school for the US Naval Academy, as they conduct a mock trial.

programme.title: JAG: Boomerang (Part 2)
programme.desc: Hostilities between Harm and Brumby escalate both in and out of the courtroom and it takes Chegwidden, with Brumby's CO's approval to get them to deal with their anger

programme.title: JAG: People V. Gunny
programme.desc: Gunny is charged with gay-bashing following an off duty altercation outside a gay bar.

programme.title: JAG: The Bridge At Kang So Ri
programme.desc: Harm and Mac board a plane bound for Korea to investigate charges that GI's shot and killed civilians during the Korean War.
programme.sub-title: The Bridge At Kang So Ri

programme.title: JAG: Drop Zone
programme.desc: Mac is prosecuting a SEAL jumpmaster charged with causing the death of one of his men during a parachute insertion exercise.

programme.title: JAG: Real Deal Seal
programme.desc: Medal of Honor Navy SEAL Lt. Curtis Rivers is court-martialed for punching a Congressional candidate who claims to be a Navy SEAL.

programme.title: JAG: Overdue And Presumed Lost
programme.desc: A salvage company tries to claim rights to the wreckage of the USS Dolphin, a WW2 submarine that was lost the day before the attack on Pearl Harbor in 1941.

programme.title: JAG: Killer Instinct
programme.desc: Louis Cellucci is having a rough time aboard the flight deck of the USS Benjamin Harrison. Moments after he walks away from another failed attempt at fixing an F-14, shouts of Man Overboard! are heard across the deck.

programme.title: JAG: To Walk on Wings
programme.desc: Congresswoman Latham and a colleague watch four marine jumpers jump into the ocean from an M22 Osprey. With two marines left to jump, the plane is struck by a swell in the ocean. Harm and Mac are instructed to investigate.

programme.title: JAG: Guilt
programme.desc: When Captain Huddleston hears that the marine corporal has been charged with rape, he sends Lt. Colonel Sarah Mac MacKenzie to handle the case in a hot zone of Anti-American activity.

programme.title: JAG: Mixed Messages
programme.desc: An intruder shoots three Navy people, killing two. One of the dead is a friend of Harm, who protects his reputation. Webb, counter-espionage, and disinformation are involved; Bud solves it. Mac and Renee return to town; Mac leaves again.

programme.title: JAG: Ambush
programme.desc: Capt. Shepard leads his troop off its peacekeeping mission and into battle with the colonel of the rebel forces. Consequently, six marines die in the public incident. Harm is sent in to investigate despite a recent touch of bad luck in a string of trials.

programme.title: JAG: Jag-a-thon
programme.desc: The JAG gang takes part in a JAGathon, a charity-benefit marathon, which provides opportunities for personal pride and individual competitiveness. Sturgis feels uncertain, and Harm defends a sailor for impersonating an officer.

programme.title: JAG: The Mission
programme.desc: A spotter on the ground calls for an airstrike as he has identified 15 heavily armed suspected Al Qaeda members on horseback. JAG won’t approve the airstrike until the spotter is absolutely certain the men are Al Qaeda members.

programme.title: JAG: Defending His Honor
programme.desc: An ambulance pulls up to a crash site where a woman, Jennifer Wilson, is found injured and crying for her baby. Unbeknownst to her, the six-month-old baby died in the accident. A Navy judge stands accused of vehicular homicide.

programme.title: JAG: In Country
programme.desc: Central Command coordinates an airstrike on a confirmed target traveling through Afghanistan. After the strike is launched, marine troops assess the damage and they are disturbed to learn that they targeted the wrong vehicle and hit civilians instead.

programme.title: JAG: Enemy Below
programme.desc: Kabir is preparing an attack against the U.S. and was last seen crossing the border from Afghanistan to Iran. He is believed to have recruited Capt. Yerastov, a Russian submarine captain. Turner, Harm and Mac help in the hunt for the terrorists.

programme.title: COPS: Cops Fighting to Lose
programme.desc: Shots fired in a park after hours leads to a messy situation for one officer, and a disturbed man with a gun takes responding officers on a harrowing chase.

programme.title: COPS
programme.desc: An out-of-control driver wants to get home for his child's birthday, officers respond to a suspected shooting, and a trip to buy dog food turns into a run-in with the police.
programme.sub-title: Cops Whose Gun Is It Anyway?

programme.title: COPS: He's the Gingerbread Man
programme.desc: A man wanted for unpaid child support hides from deputies. Officers respond to a sexual predator following a minor. A man with a checkered driving record crashes his car.

programme.title: COPS
programme.desc: A swimmer in distress is rescued during spring break. Airbnb guests are evicted in Key West. A street performer tickles the ivories and blocks pedestrians. Bikini-clad nurses are caught with booze.

programme.title: COPS: Cops Two Doors Down
programme.desc: A deputy calls for backup when he's outnumbered, a former Marine damages his neighbors' property, and a husband and wife are separated after their vehicle is searched.

programme.title: COPS: Cops Drive and Dash
programme.desc: After deputies pursue a car-theft suspect to his home, he tries to convince them he has been there all along, and a man violates a protection order to try to reconcile with his wife.

programme.title: COPS
programme.desc: Deputies halt a burglary in progress. A truck is pulled over for stolen plates and officers question how it can start with a house key. A RV is no home for a family on the brink of falling apart.

programme.title: COPS
programme.desc: A suspect on the run makes a dangerous decision when he finds himself cornered. An unsuspecting driver gets more than she bargained for when she gives an old friend a lift. A friendly woman has a thing for cowboys.

programme.title: COPS: Cops Heat of the Moment
programme.desc: A parolee leads deputies on a high-speed chase, police respond to a domestic disturbance call, and an officer helps frustrated citizens settle their differences.

programme.title: COPS
programme.desc: A wanted suspect tries to hide from officers, a woman calls deputies to apprehend her hostile husband, and a passenger tries to outrun the police during a traffic stop.

programme.title: COPS: Cops Bail Me Out, Boss
programme.desc: A distressed woman leads the cops to her abusive fiance, and a couple of officers are suspicious when they pull over an overly friendly man.

programme.title: COPS: Visiting Hours Twofer
programme.desc: Police respond to a report of prostitution in a hospital parking lot. A man and woman engaged in solicitation are pulled over. A woman in an area known for prostitution tries to evade police.

programme.title: COPS
programme.desc: A man tries to jump out of a car when his friend takes him on a high-speed chase, and a deputy investigates a man he finds walking down a dark road.

programme.title: COPS
programme.desc: A man assaults his sister and her dog, a gibberish-speaking man is arrested for attacking his neighbors, and a couple with no-contact orders tells conflicting stories.

programme.title: COPS: Cops Drive and Dash
programme.desc: After deputies pursue a car-theft suspect to his home, he tries to convince them he has been there all along, and a man violates a protection order to try to reconcile with his wife.

programme.title: COPS
programme.desc: Deputies respond to an armed carjacking, an altercation leads to a woman's arrest, and officers face a combative man.

programme.title: COPS: Yard Work
programme.desc: Deputies respond to a call of a man shooting a gun in his front yard. Officers are called to a domestic dispute over an wrinkly infant's shirt. Four armed and suspicious men are spotted by deputies.

programme.title: COPS
programme.desc: A man arguing with his father over money fights with three Deputies after an unsuccessful Taser deployment. A motorcycle runs from cops in Fontana. A suspect in Alameda County rams a patrol vehicle.

programme.title: COPS: He's the Gingerbread Man
programme.desc: A man wanted for unpaid child support hides from deputies. Officers respond to a sexual predator following a minor. A man with a checkered driving record crashes his car.

programme.title: Random Breath Test
programme.desc: A reality show on real alcohol and drug testing performed on Australian roads by the Police's RBT Squad.

programme.title: The Love Boat: Youth Takes A Holiday/ Don't Leave Home Without It/ Prisoner Of Love
programme.desc: An Army captain (Beth Howland) sets her sights on Gopher; a married couple (Charlotte Rae, Louis Nye) meet up with two swingers (Jamie Farr, Heather Locklear).
programme.sub-title: Youth Takes A Holiday/ Don't Leave Home Without It/ Prisoner Of Love

programme.title: The Love Boat: Love On Strike/ Looking For Mr. Wilson/ The Last Case
programme.desc: Everyone is a suspect when a passenger goes missing about the Pacific Princess. Meanwhile, when Julie's high-school friend boards the ship with her son, they learn an important lesson after running into the woman's father and another passenger.
programme.sub-title: Love On Strike/ Looking For Mr. Wilson/ The Last Case

programme.title: The Love Boat: Carnival Cruise/ Long Time No See/ The Bear Essence/ Kisses And Makeup
programme.desc: A carnival entrepreneur is approached on the Pacific Princess by a woman who knows a shocking secret about his past. Meanwhile, a comedy duo with a bear act competes for the affection of the same woman.
programme.sub-title: Carnival Cruise/ Long Time No See/ The Bear Essence/ Kisses And Makeup

programme.title: The Love Boat: Hong Kong Cruise: Polly'S Poker Palace - Part 1
programme.desc: On a Hong Kong cruise: a senator romances a woman until he learns the nature of her visit; two brothers date Julie and Vicki; a compulsive shopper is offered $25,000 by her husband (Herb Edelman) if she'll resist temptation during the trip.
programme.sub-title: Hong Kong Cruise: Polly'S Poker Palace - Part 1

programme.title: The Love Boat: Hong Kong Cruise: Polly'S Poker Palace - Part 2
programme.desc: An international fugitive finds love when he meets a beautiful woman with a secret, while a divorcee meets an intriguing man who conceals his identity from her and Isaac.
programme.sub-title: Hong Kong Cruise: Polly'S Poker Palace - Part 2

programme.title: The Love Boat: China Cruise: The Pledge/ East Meets West/ Dear Roberta/ My Two Dumplings - Part 2
programme.desc: A rich widow comes face to face with her estranged sister, who's working on the ship as a made and whose boyfriend the wealthy woman stole and married 40 years earlier. Meanwhile, a childless couple hatches a plan to become parents.
programme.sub-title: China Cruise: The Pledge/ East Meets West/ Dear Roberta/ My Two Dumplings - Part 2

programme.title: The Love Boat: When World's Collide/ Captain And The Geisha/ The Lottery Winners/ The Emperor's Fortune - Part 2
programme.desc: A man bound by alimony wants to make sure his ex (Michelle Phillips) goes through with her marriage; Julie's Aunt Sylvia and her pal Betsy try to interest a publisher in Betsy's memoirs.
programme.sub-title: When World's Collide/ Captain And The Geisha/ The Lottery Winners/ The Emperor's Fortune - Part 2

programme.title: The Love Boat: How Do I Love Thee...?/ No More Alimony/ Authoress! Authoress!
programme.desc: Isaac's budding relationship with a new crew member becomes complicated when Gopher tells him to fire her. Meanwhile, a congressman and his wife bring their daughter aboard the Pacific Princess after receiving letters threatening her young life.
programme.sub-title: How Do I Love Thee...?/ No More Alimony/ Authoress! Authoress!

programme.title: The Love Boat: Best Ex-Friends/ All The Congressman's Women/ Three Faces Of Love
programme.desc: A gambling cruise for charity proves a chancy thing for a teacher (McLean Stevenson), two honeymooners (Shea Farrell, Leah Ayres), and the drive chairwoman (Celeste Holm). Richard Jaeckel, Antony Ponzini, Jo Ann Pflug, Gavin MacLeod.
programme.sub-title: Best Ex-Friends/ All The Congressman's Women/ Three Faces Of Love

programme.title: The Love Boat: Ace In The Hole/ Uncle Joey's Song/ Father In The Cradle
programme.desc: Captain Stubing hires a handsome new photographer who draws the attention of two sisters who were visiting Doc and Gopher. Then, a young man is shocked to meet his mother's (Lee Meriwether) new boyfriend, who is the same age as him.
programme.sub-title: Ace In The Hole/ Uncle Joey's Song/ Father In The Cradle

programme.title: The Love Boat: A Rose Is Not A Rose / Novelties / Too Rich And Too Thin
programme.desc: An inventor's zealousness jeopardizes his chance for romance and a man's double identity upsets Julie and a passenger.
programme.sub-title: A Rose Is Not A Rose / Novelties / Too Rich And Too Thin

programme.title: The Love Boat: Side By Side/ A Fish Out Of Water/ Rub Me Tender
programme.desc: A boy who thinks his grandmother (Glynis Johns) is terminally ill will do anything to ensure her happiness; a man (Ed Begley Jr.) who's uncomfortable around women falls in love with a mermaid (Mary Crosby).
programme.sub-title: Side By Side/ A Fish Out Of Water/ Rub Me Tender

programme.title: The Love Boat: The Christmas Show: Santa, Santa, Santa/ Another Dog Gone Christmas/ Noel's Christmas Carol
programme.desc: A bevy of beauties, including a stunning new cruise director, board the Pacific Princess for an all-woman voyage to Alaska.
programme.sub-title: The Christmas Show: Santa, Santa, Santa/ Another Dog Gone Christmas/ Noel's Christmas Carol

programme.title: The Love Boat: Copenhagen/Amsterdam Cruise/ Vicki and the Fugitive - Part 1
programme.desc: Mystery abounds when a woman becomes friendly with a man she knew in a past life but he doesn't remember her. At the same time, Captain Stubing pursues a beautiful passenger who seems intent on avoiding him.
programme.sub-title: Copenhagen/Amsterdam Cruise/ Vicki and the Fugitive - Part 1

programme.title: The Love Boat: Copenhagen/Amsterdam Cruise/ Vicki and the Fugitive - Part 2
programme.desc: Isaac tries to help a recently widowed woman and her son cope with their grief and a sightless woman believes she has found her former love.
programme.sub-title: Copenhagen/Amsterdam Cruise/ Vicki and the Fugitive - Part 2

programme.title: The Love Boat: Only The Good Die Young/ Honey Beats The Odds/ The Light Of Another Day
programme.desc: A dying woman meets a man who claims he is in a debate with an angel and a woman from Gopher's past hopes that he will propose to her.
programme.sub-title: Only The Good Die Young/ Honey Beats The Odds/ The Light Of Another Day

programme.title: The Love Boat: Soap Gets In Your Eyes/ A Match Made In Heaven/ Tugs Of The Heart
programme.desc: On her 18th birthday, Vicki learns that her father is a recovering alcoholic and a woman hires a detective to find her college sweetheart.
programme.sub-title: Soap Gets In Your Eyes/ A Match Made In Heaven/ Tugs Of The Heart

programme.title: Sabrina: The Teenage Witch: The Phantom Menace
programme.desc: It’s Halloween, and Sabrina is having a ghoulish time adjusting to her new job at the coffee house.

programme.title: Sabrina: The Teenage Witch: Little Orphan Hilda
programme.desc: Hilda and Zelda experience a life-changing event when they discover they are adopted.

programme.title: Sabrina: The Teenage Witch: Spoiled Rotten
programme.desc: New charge card from the Other Realm causes Sabrina to forget that the most important things in life can’t be bought.

programme.title: Sabrina: The Teenage Witch: Aging Not So Gracefully
programme.desc: Sabrina goes a little overboard in her attempt to seem more mature for the attentions of a college boy.

programme.title: Sabrina: The Teenage Witch: Love Means Having To Say You’re Sorry
programme.desc: Paying the price for kissing Josh, Sabrina must adjust to life after Harvey.
programme.sub-title: Love Means Having To Say You’re Sorry

programme.title: Sabrina: The Teenage Witch: Ice Station Sabrina
programme.desc: A life-threatening situation forces Sabrina to risk exposing her magic to a witch hunter.

programme.title: Sabrina: The Teenage Witch: Sabrina Nipping At Your Nose
programme.desc: When the weather outside turns frightful, the Spellman’s plan a trip to Jamaica.

programme.title: Sabrina: The Teenage Witch: Now You See Her Now You Don’t
programme.desc: Adhering to a fad diet from the Other Realm, Sabrina becomes so thin she literally disappears.
programme.sub-title: Now You See Her Now You Don’t

programme.title: Sabrina: The Teenage Witch: Super Hero
programme.desc: Childhood dreams come true when Sabrina casts a spell that turns Harvey into a superhero.

programme.title: Sabrina: The Teenage Witch: Salem’s Daughter
programme.desc: As maid of honor, Sabrina pays the price for reuniting Salem with his daughter in time for her wedding.

programme.title: Sabrina: The Teenage Witch: Love In Bloom
programme.desc: Valentine’s Day gets complicated when Sabrina learns two boys are vying for her heart.

programme.title: Sabrina: The Teenage Witch: Welcome Back Duke
programme.desc: Sabrina discovers why the road to ruin is paved with good intentions.

programme.title: Sabrina: The Teenage Witch: She’s Baaack!
programme.desc: Cousin Amanda returns to give Sabrina a little competition in the teenage witch -and love-- department.

programme.title: Sabrina: The Teenage Witch: The Wild Wild Witch
programme.desc: The West gets even wilder when Sabrina takes the reins as town sheriff.

programme.title: Sabrina: The Teenage Witch: The Four Faces Of Sabrina
programme.desc: Choosing a college literally tears Sabrina apart when she tries too hard to make everyone happy.

programme.title: Sabrina: The Teenage Witch: The End Of An Era (Season Finale)
programme.desc: After four years of enduring Sabrina’s spells and strange behavior, Harvey finally suspects she is a witch
programme.sub-title: The End Of An Era (Season Finale)

programme.title: Sabrina: The Teenage Witch: Dreama, The Mouse

programme.title: Corner Gas
programme.desc: Brent explores alternatives to glasses. Hank's slogans cause trouble for Oscar.

programme.title: Pokemon: Indigo League
programme.desc: On their way to Cinnabar Island, our heroes join Officer Jenny and the police force’s Growlithe in some special training, hoping to improve Pikachu’s battling skills.
programme.sub-title: The Case of the K-9 Caper!

programme.title: Pokemon: Indigo League
programme.desc: On a brief stop for lunch, Ash, and his friends meet Todd Snap, a Pokémon photographer who has been trying to get a perfect shot of Pikachu.

programme.title: Pokemon: Indigo League
programme.desc: Realizing that he hasn’t won any Gym badges for a while, Ash signs up for the Pokémon League Admissions Exam, a great chance to prove he’s got what it takes to become a Pokémon Master!

programme.title: Pokemon: Indigo League
programme.desc: Ash and the gang decide to check out a breeding center that they saw on a commercial. However, the center is a cover for a diabolical scheme from Team Rocket members Butch and Cassidy—old rivals of Jessie and James!

programme.title: Pokemon: Adventures in the Orange Islands
programme.desc: Misty and Jessie compete to be Queen of the Princess Festival! Ash and Brock lend Misty their Pokémon for the battle, and Jessie takes a licking despite a surprise Pokémon of her own.

programme.title: Pokemon: Adventures in the Orange Islands
programme.desc: It’s Kids Day, and Ash and friends help some schoolchildren celebrate. Team Rocket spoils the fun by grabbing a kid instead of Pikachu. In a shocking turn of events, Meowth becomes a good guy…or does he?

programme.title: Pokemon: Adventures in the Orange Islands
programme.desc: Our heroes travel to Cinnabar Island, where Ash hopes to win the Volcano Badge. A strange character offers riddles about how to find the Gym, where Ash and Pikachu have a fiery battle with a Pokémon they’ve never seen before!

programme.title: Pokemon: Adventures in the Orange Islands
programme.desc: Ash has finally located the Gym, suspended above a pool of molten lava! This battle for the Volcano Badge can only be won by a strong Fire-type Pokémon, so Ash must rely on his misbehaving Charizard.

programme.title: Pokemon: Adventures in the Orange Islands
programme.desc: While Ash and the gang hurry to catch the last ferry off Cinnabar Island, they get sidetracked by a group of Squirtle, Wartortle, and Blastoise that need assistance!

programme.title: Pokemon: Adventures in the Orange Islands
programme.desc: Misty's Horsea desperately needs exercise, so the gang decides to take it to her sisters' Gym in Cerulean City. There, she learns that her sisters are planning an exciting underwater ballet.

programme.title: Pokemon: Adventures in the Orange Islands
programme.desc: Ash, Brock, and Misty get sidetracked on their way to Viridian City when all of their belongings mysteriously disappear. While searching for their missing things, they find themselves mixed up in a close encounter that’s really out of this world!

programme.title: Pokemon: Adventures in the Orange Islands
programme.desc: Our heroes make their way to Viridian City, where Ash hopes to earn the Earth Badge. They get to the Gym just in time to see his rival, Gary, being defeated by an unknown Pokémon—and Gary insists that it's evil!

programme.title: Pokemon: Adventures in the Orange Islands
programme.desc: On their way back to Pallet Town, our heroes encounter a runaway Mr. Mime. Ash disguises himself as another Mr. Mime so he can get close. When Team Rocket strikes, will they catch the right Mr. Mime?

programme.title: Pokemon: Adventures in the Orange Islands
programme.desc: Our heroes find themselves at a fork in the road and decide to climb a steep and dangerous mountain. Pikachu gets lost, and they must all work together to find it, stay warm, and weather a storm.

programme.title: Pokemon: Adventures in the Orange Islands
programme.desc: On a visit to Professor Oak’s house, Ash runs into Gary, and they talk about the upcoming Pokémon League. Professor Oak says they should learn from each other rather than competing, and that teamwork pays off when Team Rocket attacks!

programme.title: Pokemon: Adventures in the Orange Islands
programme.desc: Ash and his mother take a vacation to Seafoam Island. While he's there, he's looking for the programmer of the Pokédex to learn about the peculiar evolution of SlowPoké. Team Rocket's bumbling brings to light this surprising mystery!

programme.title: Pokemon: Adventures in the Orange Islands
programme.desc: When Ash wipes out during a surfing attempt, Victor rescues him and tells him about Humungadunga, a giant wave that sweeps through every 20 years. Can Ash and Pikachu give Victor the confidence he needs to catch the giant wave?

programme.title: Pokemon: Adventures in the Orange Islands
programme.desc: Back in Pallet Town, Ash, and friends meet Florinda, who works at the local nursery. Her Gloom won't evolve, so she’s afraid she's not training it properly. When Team Rocket attacks, everyone sees just how strong a Pokémon can become!

programme.title: Pokemon: Adventures in the Orange Islands
programme.desc: Everyone wants to become the star when a famous director arrives in town! Ash, Brock, Misty, and Team Rocket are working hard to ensure their Pokémon will be the ones to star opposite Wigglytuff in his movie as he solely desires them.

programme.title: Pokemon: Adventures in the Orange Islands
programme.desc: When the gang attends the premiere of 'Pokémon in Love,' Meowth looks back on his painful past. He shares the story of his life before Team Rocket, including how he learned to walk and talk like a human.

programme.title: Pokemon: Adventures in the Orange Islands
programme.desc: With only three weeks left before the Pokémon League competition, Ash, Brock, and Misty head up to Mt. Hideaway to find one of the Elite Four. They're after Pokémon training tips, but they discover a land of overgrown Onix instead!

programme.title: Pokemon: Adventures in the Orange Islands
programme.desc: While Ash and Brock train for the Pokémon League, they uncover a hidden temple containing Pokémon fossils. Misty remembers a hidden city where people built temples to honor Pokémon. Could they have stumbled upon the ancient city of Pokémopolis?

programme.title: Pokemon: Adventures in the Orange Islands
programme.desc: Our heroes are headed to the Pokémon League tournament at the Indigo Plateau when they meet another Trainer going their way. Unfortunately, all of his Gym badges have been stolen, so he can't compete…unless he takes Ash's badges instead!

programme.title: Pokemon: Adventures in the Orange Islands
programme.desc: The Pokémon League is about to begin with the ceremonial lighting of the torch, which carries a flame from the Legendary Pokémon Moltres. Of course, Team Rocket tries to steal it!

programme.title: Pokemon: Adventures in the Orange Islands
programme.desc: It's the first day of the Pokémon League, and when Ash's friends learn his first battle will be held on the Water Field, they're full of advice!

programme.title: Pokemon: Adventures in the Orange Islands
programme.desc: As the Pokémon League continues, Ash battles on the Rock Field and has a run-in with Team Rocket after they set up a fake Pokémon Center to lure unwary Trainers!

programme.title: Pokemon: Adventures in the Orange Islands
programme.desc: Ash faces his toughest opponent yet on the Grass Field at the Pokémon League! Are he and his Bulbasaur ready to take on his opponent and her powerful Scyther?

programme.title: Pokemon: Adventures in the Orange Islands
programme.desc: When Ash meets a like-minded Trainer named Richie at the Pokémon League, the two boys quickly become close friends—and so do their two Pikachu!

programme.title: Pokemon: Adventures in the Orange Islands
programme.desc: Ash and Richie will be paired up against each other in the fifth round of the Pokémon League! Can their friendship hold up during this significant battle?

programme.title: Pokemon: Adventures in the Orange Islands
programme.desc: Ash, Misty, and Brock are in the stands cheering for Richie as the Pokémon League winds down, and then they march together in the closing ceremonies. It's an experience that will stick with Ash throughout his journey as a Trainer!

programme.title: Pokemon: Adventures in the Orange Islands
programme.desc: Ash and friends return to Pallet Town for a hero's welcome, only to be sent on another journey right away! Professor Oak is seeking a mysterious-looking Poké Ball, and he asks Ash, Brock, and Misty to find it for him.

programme.title: Pokemon: Adventures in the Orange Islands
programme.desc: Our heroes prepare for their long journey to Valencia Island to bring back a mysterious Poké Ball for Professor Oak. When Ash wins a free blimp ride, it seems their travel woes are no more—but who's flying this thing?

programme.title: Pokemon: Adventures in the Orange Islands
programme.desc: After making a crash landing onto Valencia Island, our heroes get directions to Professor Ivy’s laboratory, where the mysterious Poké Ball is being held. Brock is delighted to discover that the professor and her colleagues are fellow Pokémon breeders.

programme.title: Pokemon: Adventures in the Orange Islands
programme.desc: Thanks to Jigglypuff's sleep-inducing song, the blimp carrying Ash and Misty back home goes off course and crashes on an island, where they meet a new friend named Tracey while trying to help a lost Lapras.

programme.title: Pokemon: Adventures in the Orange Islands
programme.desc: Ash, Misty, and Tracey set out to explore the Orange Islands, and Ash is determined to qualify for the Orange League! He soon learns that the Orange Crew challenges are a little different from the usual Gym battles.

programme.title: Pokemon: Adventures in the Orange Islands
programme.desc: When Ash, Misty, and Tracey arrive at Mandarin Island, Pikachu and Togepi turn against them and join a group of other angry Pokémon. When the misbehaving Pokémon disappear, Officer Jenny informs them that dozens of Pokémon have been reported missing!

programme.title: Pokemon: Adventures in the Orange Islands
programme.desc: Ash discovers a message in a bottle, which prompts our heroes to embark on a new journey on Sunburst Island and a quest for a highly elusive Onix!

programme.title: Sister, Sister: History A La Cart
programme.desc: An embarrassing arrest from his college days comes back to haunt Ray, and Lisa turns an inheritance into a shopping spree

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Grandpa Campbell
programme.desc: When Ray's ne'er-do-well father comes for a visit, he turns the household upside-down -- and turns Tamera into a ruthless businesswoman!

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Twins Get Fired
programme.desc: When Tia becomes Tamera's new boss at Rocket Burger, they both learn a lesson in responsibility

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Halloween
programme.desc: While the twins go in search of a Halloween rave, Lisa and Ray attend a fancy party thrown by a prospective client.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Weird Science
programme.desc: Tia and Tamera think they're in for a "free ride" when Ray starts dating their science teacher.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Thanksgiving in Hawaii (Part I)
programme.desc: When Ray and Lisa take the girls to Hawaii for Thanksgiving, they're forced to share their condo with their "exes," who have begun dating each other.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Thanksgiving in Hawaii (Part II)
programme.desc: While Ray and Lisa frantically search for the twins, who are missing in Hawaii, Roger opens the Campbell/Landry home to hobos for Thanksgiving dinner.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Private School
programme.desc: The prospect of Tia's attending a prestigious prep school puts social pressure on Tia and financial pressure on Lisa.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Christmas
programme.desc: When a loan shark comes after Ray's father for a bad debt, the family comes to his rescue.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Double Double Date
programme.desc: The twins find themselves in double trouble when they switch identities and Tia winds up with two dates for the same party.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Reality Really Bites
programme.desc: For her film class, Tia "spices up" her family documentary by throwing all sorts of monkey wrenches into their lives.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: The Volunteers
programme.desc: While volunteering at a local retirement home, Tamera becomes determined to help a grumpy old man find happiness again.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Valentines Day
programme.desc: Valentine's Day finds Tamera trying to "steal back" her hot date, and Ray and Lisa wondering if the other person is in love with them.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Paper or Plastic?
programme.desc: Tamera crosses the picket lines to work at a supermarket, but soon realizes that being a "scab" isn't all it's cracked up to be.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: The Piano Lesson
programme.desc: Ray learns a "noteworthy" lesson in honesty when he "fibs" and tells a tone-deaf Tamera that she plays the piano well, and she winds up humiliating herself at a piano recital.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Summer Bummer
programme.desc: Tia and Tamera trade places so that Tia can be with a cute guy in summer school and Tamera can "goof off" by taking Tia's spot as a camp counselor.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: The Candidate
programme.desc: Tamera learns how much she needs Tia's help when she runs for class president.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Big Twin on Campus
programme.desc: When Tia poses as a college student to impress her older boyfriend, Tamera goes to great lengths to fit in with Tia's new circle of "mature" friends.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: The Audition
programme.desc: Tamera's budding career as a lowdown blues singer is threatened when she gets "too happy" after her dream date asks her out!

programme.title: Sister, Sister: When a Man Loves Two Women
programme.desc: Unbeknownst to the twins, they've both got a crush on the same guy: Roger!
programme.sub-title: When a Man Loves Two Women

programme.title: Sister, Sister: You Are So Beautiful
programme.desc: Curiosity about their birth parents leads the twins, Ray, and Lisa on a quest to learn more about the girls' past.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Gimme a Brake
programme.desc: Tempted by the freedom of a new car, Tia and Tamera soon find themselves on a collision course with disaster when their car drops off a dock and into Lake Erie!

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Daddys Girl
programme.desc: Tamera learns she's never too old to be a daddy's girl when she finds herself jealous of the growing closeness between Ray and Tia.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Sis Boom Bah
programme.desc: In a bid to boost their popularity at school, the twins try out for the cheerleading squad -- and Ray tries his hand at coaching them.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Kid-Napped
programme.desc: When a new girl in school threatens Tia and Tamera's popularity, the twins join forces to win back their friends with the help of a music superstar.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Boy From the Hood
programme.desc: Tamera falls for Tia's childhood friend Darnell, who's all grown up and running with a fast crowd.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: I'll be There
programme.desc: Tia takes a job in a '50s diner to get away from Tamera and is shocked when Tamera starts working there, too.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Ch-Ch-Ch-Changes
programme.desc: The twins are thrilled when Ray and Lisa decide to take their relationship to a more romantic level, but have second thoughts when Roger outlines the downsides of their parents dating.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Double Exposure
programme.desc: When Tia poses for Tamera's class picture, the gorgeous photo that results makes Tamera extremely popular, and Tia extremely resentful.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Some Like it Hockey
programme.desc: Determined to make the school's all-male hockey team, Tia and Tamera must disguise themselves as boys and pretend they're just two of the guys!

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Bring on Debate
programme.desc: Ray feels left out by the close bond Tamera forms with her new music teacher.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Little Man Date
programme.desc: Tia and her new beau Kyle decide to fix up Tamera with Kyle's little brother – who turns out to be a lot littler than anyone thought!

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Ski Squad
programme.desc: A ski trip designed to give the twins some independence from their parents has them longing for the comforts of home after they're trapped in an avalanche.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Cafeteria Lady
programme.desc: Lisa gets a new job in the high school cafeteria, but her outrageous behavior proves to be a major embarrassment to Tia.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Three the Heart Way
programme.desc: Dateless Tamera feels like a third wheel when Tia and Roger get matched up on Valentine's Day.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Model Tia
programme.desc: The family flashes back to the Disco Decade when Ray and Lisa attend a 1970s theme party, while Tamera falls prey to a man who promises to launch her modeling career.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: My Guy
programme.desc: A high school nerd out to woo Tia turns to Tamera for help in updating his image, but when his makeover is complete, Tamera realizes she wants to keep him for herself!

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Double Dutch
programme.desc: When a girl from Tia's old neighborhood challenges her to a double Dutch jump rope contest, Tia becomes obsessed with winning.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Laa-Laa comes in through the door and up the slide to join the others and watch Christopher whose mum and dad look after poorly hedgehogs. A magical new event happens in Teletubbyland – Little Bo Peep has lost her sheep.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Laa-Laa jumps indoors and then runs off to watch some children sing and act out the nursery rhyme 'Old King Cole'. In Teletubbyland, the Teletubbies act out their own version of 'Old King Cole'.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: A voice trumpet rises to tell the Teletubbies ‘Christmas is a Coming’. They hurry up the slide to see some children decorate a Christmas Tree. The Teletubbies get excited when their own Christmas Tree appears in Teletubbyland.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Bryony loves to play the violin. She talks about her violin and they plays 'Twinkle Twinkle Little Star'. The Teletubbies sit down to listen to some lovely music, but the Noo Noo is making too much noise.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies play a swapping places game among the hills. They watch a little girl paint a portrait of her mum. A book appears and Tinky Winky looks at the pictures in the book.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Dipsy is busy at the switch panel, when he remembers the snow. The Tubbies watch an international Christmas insert. The Teletubbies are walking very carefully because snow can be very slippery.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies play with their favourite things, until the windmill shows them it's time to watch some children learn about the number one. The Teletubbies are supposed to be in bed, but they would rather play outside.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies play a game before watching some children playing a game with Alex Pascall. The Teletubbies have fun playing the game of waking and sleeping, before Tubby Bye-Bye.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies watch as Andy Brown shows some children butterflies. The Teletubbies love to fly their kites and a strong wind blows Po all over Teletubbyland.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Some children learn about the number four, and the Teletubbies decide to go into the house. One by one, they leave. So, who is in the house?

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Tubbies watch a little girl have her hair braided by her mum. They put different coloured bands in her hair. In Teletubbyland, Tinky Winky can't sleep.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies giggle as they stand up and sit down to some music. They watch some children perform a 'Jack in the Box' dance.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Five voice trumpets rises and plays some lovely music for the Teletubbies, who dance around Home Hill. Two girls have brought some new Tropical fish.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Tinky Winky and Laa-Laa burst out of the house, dance, have a big hug and go back inside, they do it again until the windmill announces the animals marching through Teletubbyland. Dipsy and Po burst out of the house, dance, have a big hug.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: A voice trumpet brings a message for the Teletubbies. The Teletubbies watch some children feeding the ducks. Po finds a new trick to play with Tubby Toast. Three ships sail through Teletubbyland.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Some children recite and act out a nursery rhyme. In Teletubbyland Po follows Tinky Winky, they go over the hills, along the path, between the trees, into the house, around the toaster and into bed.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies dance a twisty dance, then they watch two children rollerblading in New York. The Noo Noo tidies up Tinky Winky's Tubby Toast, so Tinky Winky chases him round and round Home Hill.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies pop up from behind the hill and rush off to see a little Indian boy playing on a drum called a Tabla. In Teletubbyland, it's time for Tubby Toast.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies run indoors and then run out again to watch Andy Brown and Georgina wash a baby elephant. Laa-Laa and Dipsy have a messy accident.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Three Teletubbies dance through Teletubbyland. The Teletubbies watch some children learn about the number three. Dipsy climbs a steep hill.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Some voice trumpets rise to wish the Tubbies good morning. The Teletubbies watch some children herding sheep. The Teletubbies go in and out, and up and down, round the corner and along to top until it is time for Tubby Custard.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Tubbies watch two boys having their hair cut. A mirror appears in Teletubbyland. Laa-Laa, Dipsy and Tinky Winky liked looking at their reflection in the mirror.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies watch a group of children washing their goat in the river. In Teletubbyland it’s time for Tubby Custard. Tinky Winky, Dipsy and Laa-Laa spilled custer on themselves.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies watch some children making marks. They use paint and different objects to make a colourful picture.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Tubbies love to run round and round Teletubbyland, but stop to watch three children make some modelling dough to play with. The Tubby Toaster makes a big piece of Toast.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies watch some children crawling. In Teletubbyland, the Teletubbies are crawling. Tinky Winky crawled along the path, Dipsy crawled over the hill, Laa-Laa crawled between the trees and Po crawled around the flowers.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies giggle together as they hold hands and side step around Home Hill. They watch three children build a jigsaw puzzle and try to guess what the picture is.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies and a group of children see Tamzin Griffin pretend to be Funny Lady and tell the story of the naughty hungry horse. In Teletubbyland, Po is playing with Laa-Laa's ball, she doesn't want to give it back until she sees her scooter.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Tubbies dance a Bumps-a-daisy dance and then watch some children decorating lots of boxes to make a big caterpillar. A Jack in a Box appears in Teletubbyland.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies pop up and run off to watch some children tell a story with their drawings. Laa-Laa goes for a walk and as she walks she sings a song about what she sees.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Po and Tinky Winky love to smell flowers. They have great fun running between two groups of flowers smelling them with great big sniffs.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies love playing "Boo!" The Teletubbies watch some children learn about the number six. Tinky Winky, Dipsy and Laa-Laa get their knees dirty and have fun getting them clean.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies watch two children and their goats. The dancing Teddy bear appears in Teletubbyland. The Teletubbies sing a song.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Teletubbies love to run and do exercises. The Tubbies watch a girl and a boy throwing a ball. Then they play a game together with the ball.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Tinky Winky, Dipsy and Laa-Laa sit down to eat some Tubby Custard. Po wants some too. She tries to eat the other's Tubby Custard, but they won't let her.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies watch a little girl whose Mum has a very special job. Po has too much Tubby Custard and Tubby Custard Tummy and there's time foe one more dance before Tubby Bye-Bye.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: A cloud appears in Teletubbyland. The Teletubbies watch some children playing in the rain. Dipsy steps in a puddle. Three magic ships sail through Teletubbyland.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Clare Wood
programme.desc: In April 2007, Clare Wood met George Appleton on a dating website and they embarked on a relationship together. Unbeknownst to her, Appleton was a criminal with a severe history of violence against women.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Peter Sutcliffe
programme.desc: Arguably Britain's most infamous serial killer, Peter Sutcliffe became known as The Yorkshire Ripper when he began murdering women in Yorkshire during the 1970s.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: The London Nail Bombings
programme.desc: London was brought to a standstill in April 1999 when 23-year-old political extremist David Copeland caused terror with a bombing campaign.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Joanne Dennehy
programme.desc: Dermot Murnaghan returns to uncover the truth behind the horrific crimes of Peterborough serial killer Joanna Dennehy.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Rochdale Groomers
programme.desc: Rochdale Groomers: Documentary examining the sex abuse scandal which occurred in the North of England and appeared to go undetected for quite some time.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Keith Blakelock
programme.desc: In 1985, PC Keith Blakelock was murdered during a riot on a housing estate in London, triggered after a black woman died of heart failure during a house search. Nobody has been brought to justice for his murder, despite three investigations.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: The Marchioness Disaster
programme.desc: The story of the Marchioness; a pleasure boat that collided with dredger Bowbelle and sank within minutes resulting in the loss of 51 lives.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Garry Newlove
programme.desc: Garry Newlove’s wife Helen talks about the events on the night of his murder and the days afterwards, as well as her current work as a peer in the House of Lords combatting anti-social behaviour.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Shipman
programme.desc: Doctor Harold Shipman was convicted of killing 15 patients in 2000, but he is thought to have killed up to 215. Discover how he was eventually caught by detectives.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: The Murder Of Rhys Jones
programme.desc: The parents of innocent 11-year-old Rhys Jones, shot on his way home from a football club in Liverpool 2007, speak about the harrowing day when they lost their son.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Steven Wright
programme.desc: A closer look at the crimes of Steve Wright, who was dubbed 'The Suffolk Strangler' after murdering five women who worked as prostitutes in Ipswich in 2006.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Raoul Moat
programme.desc: The hunt for Raoul Moat was one of the largest in British history, after he wounded his ex-girlfriend, killed her new partner and shot and blinded police constable David Rathband.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Jimmy Savile
programme.desc: Jimmy Savile: From national treasure to one of the UK's most notorious sex offenders: this is the story of how the revelation of Jimmy Savile's crimes left the UK reeling.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Sarah Payne
programme.desc: Eight-year-old Sarah Payne's disappearance and murder shocked the nation. Here, Sarah's mum reveals her tireless campaign to protect other children from paedophilia.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Colin Ireland
programme.desc: In the summer of 1993, gay men in London were being terrorised by a brutal killer. Colin Ireland, a loner from Southend, had made a macabre resolution – to become a serial killer.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: The Philpotts
programme.desc: When six children died in a house fire in Allenton, Derby in May 2012 it shocked the nation. Parents Mick and Mairead Philpott were charged with the murders. We uncover the reasons behind the arson attack on their own home.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Stephen Lawrence
programme.desc: On the 20th anniversary of his death, the family of Stephen Lawrence reflect on his tragic murder in 1993, as well as their lengthy fight for justice.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Lee Rigby
programme.desc: In broad daylight on a busy Woolwich street on 22nd May 2013, serving soldier Fusilier Lee Rigby was attacked and murdered by Islamic extremists Michael Adebolajo and Michael Adebowale.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Stephanie Slater
programme.desc: Estate agent Stephanie Slater reveals the story of her abduction, rape and incarceration, which occurred in 1992 whilst showing a man around a house.

programme.title: Girlfriends: Santa V. Monica
programme.desc: William’s ambitious girlfriend has big plans that are alienating him from Joan, Lynn, Maya, and Toni.

programme.title: Girlfriends: Take This Poem and Call Me in the Morning
programme.desc: While Ellis mopes about his career, Lynn lies about her past.
programme.sub-title: Take This Poem and Call Me in the Morning

programme.title: Girlfriends: Howdy Partner
programme.desc: Joan and William are in for a big shock when their boss announces the name of the firm’s new partner.

programme.title: Girlfriends: Happy Valentine's Day… Baby?
programme.desc: Secrets and surprises are in store for Joan, William, Toni, and Lynn on Valentine’s Day.

programme.title: Girlfriends: Sex, Lies and Books
programme.desc: William tells Monica the truth, Maya’s divorce sends her into the arms of another, and Joan hosts a snooty book club.

programme.title: Girlfriends: A Stiff Good Man is Easy to Find
programme.desc: William’s girlfriend encourages him to cheat on her, and Toni can’t decide whether to marry Todd or dump him.
programme.sub-title: A Stiff Good Man is Easy to Find

programme.title: Girlfriends: Runaway Bridesmaid
programme.desc: Ellis suspects Joan’s unhappiness has more to do with Toni’s wedding than their relationship.

programme.title: Girlfriends: Single Mama Drama
programme.desc: Maya makes a new friend that her old friends don’t like, and Lynn fakes her birthday to collect on a cash prize.

programme.title: Girlfriends: The Pact
programme.desc: Joan’s former romantic rival drops a bombshell, and William’s former boss dangles a tantalizing offer in his face.

programme.title: Girlfriends: Where Everyone Knows My Name
programme.desc: Maya is upset after she sees Darnell with a new woman.

programme.title: Girlfriends: Too Much Sharkin
programme.desc: Joan blows the lid off William’s office romance.

programme.title: Girlfriends: The Wedding, Part I
programme.desc: The beginning of Toni’s life with Todd could spell the end of her friendship with Joan.

programme.title: Girlfriends: The Wedding, Part II
programme.desc: Toni and Joan confront crises with their men – and each other.

programme.title: Girlfriends: Blood is Thicker Than Liquor
programme.desc: A family crisis erupts on the eve of Toni and Todd’s wedding.

programme.title: Girlfriends: The Fast Track and the Furious
programme.desc: Maya goes to work for William.
programme.sub-title: The Fast Track and the Furious

programme.title: Girlfriends: Some Enchanted Evening
programme.desc: Joan finds her soul mate. Too bad it’s not her boyfriend.

programme.title: Girlfriends: If It’s Broke, Fix It
programme.desc: Toni’s husband is hiding a costly secret.

programme.title: Girlfriends: Snoop, There It Is
programme.desc: Lynn snoops into Sivad’s past.

programme.title: Girlfriends: You Ain't Gotta Go Home, But…You know The Rest!
programme.desc: Toni’s brother, Antoine, visits Maya – and refuses to leave
programme.sub-title: You Ain't Gotta Go Home, But…You know The Rest!

programme.title: Girlfriends: Hopelessly Devoted to Two
programme.desc: Ellis’ return forces Joan to confront her feelings for Brock.

programme.title: Girlfriends: Inherit the Lynn
programme.desc: Lynn moves in with Maya.

programme.title: Girlfriends: And Baby Makes Four
programme.desc: William has second thoughts about fatherhood.

programme.title: Girlfriends: Viva Las Vegas
programme.desc: Joan hears wedding bells – but are they all in her mind?

programme.title: Girlfriends: Between Brock and a Hard Place
programme.desc: Brock shocks Joan with news that could end their engagement.
programme.sub-title: Between Brock and a Hard Place

programme.title: Girlfriends: Don't You Want Me Baby?
programme.desc: When Toni revisits the past, it could destroy her future with Todd.

programme.title: Girlfriends: Merry Ex-Mas
programme.desc: Maya claims she’s over Darnell – but is she?

programme.title: Girlfriends: Prophet and Loss
programme.desc: Joan and William are haunted by the words of a stranger who’s either a psychic – or a psycho.

programme.title: Girlfriends: A Comedy of Eros
programme.desc: Valentine’s Day finds Toni meddling in Joan’s love life, and William hoping to make a date with destiny

programme.title: Girlfriends: Leggo My Ego
programme.desc: Toni’s obsessed with meeting supermodel Beverly Johnson.

programme.title: Girlfriends: Good Catch or a Bad Hop?
programme.desc: Maya fears her major-league beau is a real player.
programme.sub-title: Good Catch or a Bad Hop?

programme.title: Girlfriends: On the Couch
programme.desc: Toni must choose between her husband and her mother.

programme.title: Girlfriends: Love, Peace & Hair Grease
programme.desc: Joan’s in for a big surprise when she contemplates quitting her job

programme.title: Girlfriends: Wieners and Losers
programme.desc: After quitting the law firm, Joan goes from the courtrooms to food courts.

programme.title: Girlfriends: He Loves Her, He Loves Me Not
programme.desc: William’s new girlfriend claims he is in love with Joan.
programme.sub-title: He Loves Her, He Loves Me Not

programme.title: Girlfriends: A Partnerless Partner
programme.desc: William’s career may be in high gear, but his love life is stuck in park.

programme.title: Girlfriends: Just the Three of Us
programme.desc: Toni’s breakthrough in therapy could push her marriage to the breaking point

programme.title: Girlfriends: Love Thy Neighbor
programme.desc: While Lynn and Maya face off over a man, Toni fights to save her marriage.

programme.title: Girlfriends: New York Bound (2 part)

programme.desc: Toni’s friends think she’s lying about her pregnancy, and Maya causes trouble for Reverend Al Sharpton.

programme.title: Hunter: The Black Dahlia
programme.desc: Detective Doyle comes out of retirement to help Hunter and McCall investigate the real-life murder of actress-turned-prostitute Elizabeth Short, who was cut in half 41 years before.

programme.title: Hunter: Not Just Another John Doe
programme.desc: When Hunter and McCall investigate the murder of an unidentified man, their interest is piqued because the victim seemingly never existed -- and the killing appears to be a professional murder.

programme.title: Hunter: Naked Justice - Part 1
programme.desc: Hunter and McCall investigate the mysterious death of a famous movie actress and encounter a surviving sister hungry for the inheritance.

programme.title: Hunter: Naked Justice - Part 2
programme.desc: Hunter, investigating the murder of a famous movie actress, follows a contract killer to Mexico and learns why the CIA is interested in the killing.

programme.title: Hunter: Girl on the Beach
programme.desc: Captain Devane tries to take Hunter and McCall off the investigation of his ex-wife's murder and violates his forced leave of absence to pursue the killer himself.

programme.title: Hunter: Bagota's Million
programme.desc: A Colombian drug dealer is murdered and police informant Sporty James becomes the prime suspect, hunted by both Hunter and the criminals.

programme.title: Hunter: Death Signs
programme.desc: Hunter and McCall's investigation of the stabbing of a deaf man hinges on cryptic clues the victim gave in sign language before he died.

programme.title: Hunter: Boomerang
programme.desc: The wife of a businessman who was killed in an explosion on his yacht becomes the prime suspect when Hunter and McCall discover that she was recently removed from his will.

programme.title: Hunter: The Fourth Man
programme.desc: Hunter and McCall investigate the whereabouts of four million dollars still missing from a six-year-old drug bust that implicates the police in criminal activity.

programme.title: Hunter: Murder He Wrote
programme.desc: Hunter and McCall investigate the death of a multi-millionaire, whose four heirs become prime suspects.

programme.title: Hunter: Silver Bullet
programme.desc: The only clue in a series of murders is a trail of police-issued ammunition that leads Hunter and McCall to suspect a colleague as the killer.

programme.title: Hunter
programme.desc: A boy is the only witness to the shooting of his parents. As McCall attempts to unravel the circumstances surrounding the murders, Hunter must get the boy to talk about the shocking incident.

programme.title: Hunter: The Baby Game
programme.desc: Hunter and McCall enter the world of baby brokering when the murdered body of a call girl and an unharmed two-year-old are left in front of the woman's television set.

programme.title: Hunter: Presumed Guilty
programme.desc: McCall finds her loyalties sharply divided when an important murder case being prosecuted by the Assistant District Attorney, with whom she is in love, is jeopardized by Hunter.

programme.title: Hunter: Dead on Target - Part 1
programme.desc: While investigating the apparent suicide of Hunter's friend from the Vietnam War, the pair uncovers a 20-year-old betrayal that is still killing people today.

programme.title: Hunter: Dead on Target - Part 2
programme.desc: While investigating the apparent suicide of Hunter's friend from his days in Vietnam, the pair uncovers a 20-year-old betrayal that is still killing people today.

programme.title: Hunter: No Good Deed Ever Goes Unpunished
programme.desc: Mysterious tape recordings accusing a company of illegally dumping toxic waste seem like a prank until Hunter and McCall find that someone is willing to kill to conceal the truth.
programme.sub-title: No Good Deed Ever Goes Unpunished

programme.title: Hunter: Honorable Profession
programme.desc: When Hunter confronts the police department to defend the actions of a Chinese-American policewoman critically wounded in the line of duty, the pair heads to Chinatown.

programme.title: Hunter: Payback
programme.desc: Hunter and McCall try to solve a bank robbery and murder while coping with the double and triple-crossing schemes of a criminal who once saved Hunter's life.

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: On Graham's sofa are movie royalty Sir Michael Caine and Morgan Freeman, both starring in `Going in Style', comedian Jack Whitehall and Gemma Whelan from `Game of Thrones' who are in BBC drama `Decline and Fall', and Take That.

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: On Graham's sofa are Warren Beatty talking about `Rules Don't Apply', Miranda Hart starring in `Annie', Keeley Hawes in TV series `The Durrells', and Peter Capaldi discusses his decision to leave `Doctor Who'. Plus, music from Jennifer Hudson.

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: On Graham's sofa are Harry Styles giving an exclusive UK television performance of his debut solo single `Sign of the Times', Irish funnyman Brendan O'Carroll, star of `Line of Duty' Vicky McClure, and Rob Brydon talking about `The Trip to Spain'.

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: On Graham's sofa are Goldie Hawn and Amy Schumer together in action comedy `Snatched', Hollywood star Orlando Bloom, and John Boyega appearing in `Woyzeck' at the Old Vic. Plus, Lucie Jones performs the UK's Eurovision entry `Never Give Up on You'.

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: On the sofa are Diane Keaton talking about the movie `Hampstead', Jessica Chastain in `Miss Sloane', Kevin Bacon in the series `I Love Dick', and Michael Fassbender reprising his role in `Alien: Covenant'. Plus, Gorillaz perform `We Got the Power'.

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: Guy Ritchie and Charlie Hunnam talk about `King Arthur: Legend of the Sword', Billie Piper talks `Yerma' at the Young Vic, and comedian Jason Manford talks about `Bigheads'. Plus, Imelda May performs `Should've Been You'.

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The guests on the sofa are Nicole Kidman starring in Civil War drama `The Beguiled', musician Keith Urban, and theatre and screen star Alan Cumming talking about his memoir `You Gotta Get Bigger Dreams'. Plus, Sheryl Crow who performs `Be Myself'.

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: On Graham's sofa are Liam Payne who chats and sings `Strip That Down', Salma Hayek in `Beatriz at Dinner', comedian and writer David Walliams talking about `The World's Worst Children 2', and the stars of `White Gold' Ed Westwick and James Buckley.

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: Graham is joined by Tom Cruise and Annabelle Wallis, starring in `The Mummy', and Zac Efron, promoting the movie revival of `Baywatch'. Plus, singer-songwriter Beth Ditto performs her single `Fire'.

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: Graham is joined by heavyweight boxer Anthony Joshua, Rachel Weisz starring in `My Cousin Rachel', the hilarious Greg Davies, Martin Freeman talking about his West End political comedy `Labour of Love', and a musical performance from Shawn Mendes.

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: On the sofa are Mark Wahlberg in `Transformers: The Last Knight', Tom Holland in `Spider-Man: Homecoming', Sienna Miller in `Cat on a Hot Tin Roof', Woody Harrelson and Andy Serkis in `War for the Planet of the Apes', and Alison Moyet.

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: Graham is joined by Dame Judi Dench who plays Queen Victoria in new film `Victoria and Abdul', Steve Carell and Kristen Wiig together in `Despicable Me 3', and Jamie Foxx who stars in musical caper `Baby Driver'. Plus, HAIM perform `Want You Back'.

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: Graham is joined by Harrison Ford and Ryan Gosling, who star together in `Blade Runner 2049', Margot Robbie, who is starring in `Goodbye Christopher Robin', and Reese Witherspoon, who stars in romantic comedy `Home Again'. Plus music from Bananarama.

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: Joining Graham on the sofa are Kate Winslet and Idris Elba, who star together in romance-disaster movie `The Mountain Between Us', and stand-up comedian Chris Rock. Plus music and chat from Liam Gallagher who performs single `For What It's Worth'.

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: Graham is joined by actor and activist Jane Fonda, Nicole Kidman and Colin Farrell, who star together in `The Killing of a Sacred Deer', Bryan Cranston, who is performing at the National Theatre, and comedian Matt Lucas. With music from Niall Horan.

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: Graham Norton is joined by American politician Hillary Clinton, who discusses `What Happened', actor Jeff Goldblum, who is in `Thor: Ragnarok', actor Gerard Butler, who is in `Geostorm', and comedian Jack Whitehall. Plus music from Gregory Porter.

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: Joining Graham on the sofa are actors Emma Thompson and Adam Sandler, who appear together in `The Meyerowitz Stories', Claire Foy, star of `The Crown', and model, actor and writer Cara Delevingne. Plus a performance by Morrissey.

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: Graham Norton is joined by Judi Dench and Kenneth Branagh, who star in the new movie version of `Murder on the Orient Express', Johnny Depp, Michelle Pfeiffer and Josh Gad. Plus music from St Vincent, who performs the single `Los Ageless'.

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: Graham Norton is joined by Hugh Grant, appearing in `Paddington 2', Jason Momoa, starring as Aquaman in `Justice League', comedian Sarah Millican, and triple Grammy winner Kelly Clarkson, who performs `Love So Soft' from her album `Meaning of Life'.

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: Joining Graham are `Strictly' head judge Shirley Ballas and the stars of `Daddy's Home 2', Mel Gibson, Will Ferrell, Mark Wahlberg and John Lithgow. With music from Kesha, who performs `Learn to Let Go'.

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: Together on Graham's sofa are rock star Sir Elton John, actor and writer Stephen Fry, actress Carey Mulligan, pop star Robbie Williams, singer-songwriter P!nk, who performs `What About Us'.

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: Joining Graham are actress Jessica Chastain, comedian and writer Dawn French, actress Rebel Wilson, and the stars of `Jumanji: Welcome to the Jungle', Dwayne Johnson, Kevin Hart and Jack Black. With music from Noel Gallagher's High Flying Birds.

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: Among the guests are the stars of the film `Star Wars: The Last Jedi': Daisy Ridley, John Boyega, Gwendoline Christie, and Luke Skywalker himself: Mark Hamill. Also joining Graham is award-winning singer-songwriter Sam Smith, performing `Pray'.

programme.title: Earth: Final Conflict: Horizon Zero
programme.desc: A high profile mission to Mars is suddenly canceled, much to the dismay of the public. Involved air force captain Paul Chandler steals Lili's shuttle.

programme.title: Earth: Final Conflict: Scopions Dream
programme.desc: Dr. Larry Clark is a bio engineer who works on the skrills. When the companions decide to get rid of a series, one of them joins with Clark.

programme.title: Earth: Final Conflict: Live Free or Die
programme.desc: Navy Seal major Raymond McIntyre kidnaps Da'an and demands to know where his men are. They were involved in a inter-dimensional space flight test that went wrong.

programme.title: Earth: Final Conflict: The Scarecrow Returns
programme.desc: Research continues on the 'scarecrow' probe. Boone starts learning Taelon to interpret its messages. The device meanwhile absorbs a scientist, Rayna.

programme.title: Earth: Final Conflict: Sandoval's Run
programme.desc: Sandoval's CVI is breaking down. While Dr. Belman tries to remove it before implanting a new one, Sandoval escapes. He wants to rescue his wife DeeDee.

programme.title: Earth: Final Conflict: The Secret of Standhill
programme.desc: An Irish guide finds the possible burial location of Ma'el, a Taelon that came to Earth 2000 years ago. It is crucial for the Taelons to find his research material.

programme.title: Earth: Final Conflict: Pandora's Box
programme.desc: While Boone is haunted by hallucinations of captain Lucas Johnson, the Taelon Rho'ha is to undergo a experiment of experiencing human emotions.

programme.title: Earth: Final Conflict: If You Could Read my Mind
programme.desc: The Taelon Commonality is invaded and psychic Katya Petrenko is the prime suspect. She tells Boone she knows about his secret activities and needs him.
programme.sub-title: If You Could Read my Mind

programme.title: Earth: Final Conflict: Wrath of Achilles
programme.desc: The resistance releases a virus of Augur on the Taelon headquarters in Washington. Things get out of hand.

programme.title: Earth: Final Conflict: The Devil You Know
programme.desc: While a dying colonel implants his brain in a deceased criminal, the Taelons show a new electromagnetic pulse weapon. Jonathan Doors wishes to acquire it.

programme.title: Earth: Final Conflict: Law and Order
programme.desc: The Synod agrees to let Rho'ha be charged with murder in a human court. He will be prosecuted by Joshua Doors, the son of Jonathan Doors.

programme.title: Earth: Final Conflict: Through the Looking Glass
programme.desc: A new inter-dimensional portal system for human travel turns out to be something more when the resistance discover a CVI in a boy who got lost in the system.

programme.title: Earth: Final Conflict: Infection
programme.desc: Ne'eg is killed by a bacteria that is both deadly to Taelons and Humans. If it spreads, it might be disastrous. A new racist group attacks a community center.

programme.title: Earth: Final Conflict: Destruction
programme.desc: The Liberation sends a team to destroy the scarecrow probe before the Taelons crack it. Joshua Doors is appointed as secretary of Human-Taelon relations.

programme.title: Earth: Final Conflict: The Joining
programme.desc: An excavation team unleashes a very dangerous Taelon on Earth. Siobhan Beckett comes over to America. She suspects Lili of being a spy and investigates.

programme.title: Earth: Final Conflict: First of its Kind
programme.desc: Following Boone's confrontation with Hegel that left him severely injured, he was eliminated by Zo'or. Beckett who was impregnated by Hegel gives birth to a boy who immediately grows up to a man.

programme.title: Earth: Final Conflict: Avatus
programme.desc: Da'an looses his contact with the the Taelon Commonality. This causes him to change and Zo'or instructs that Da'an must be killed; because he's now considered a threat.

programme.title: Earth: Final Conflict: A Stitch in Time
programme.desc: An exhibition for the first arrival of the Taelons showcases different artifacts. An artifact causes Liam to travel to an alternate time. Zo'or questions how Da'an was able to rejoin the Commonality.

programme.title: Earth: Final Conflict: Dimensions
programme.desc: Liam and Augur are in a Taelon shuttle craft, when something happens they get thrown into a parallel universe. They discover that humanity is at war with the Taelons.

programme.title: Earth: Final Conflict: Moonscape
programme.desc: Augur is forced to take part in a Taelon experiment. Da'an and Liam try to convince Zo'or that altering minds and implanting humans to make them aggressive to fight the Jaridians is not the correct path. Maiya makes contact with Sandoval.

programme.title: Earth: Final Conflict: The Sleepers
programme.desc: A Jaridian probe lands on Earth and begins sucking energy from the Comonality. The Taelons use humans in a near-death state to keep their connection while Lili and Sandoval try to find out what is draining the energy.
